meta.scene_summary: We're building the future on nuclear-powered hardware just to watch AI agents break it in real-time.

cooking.headline: Meta is buying enough nuclear power to make your utility bill feel personally attacked
cooking.summary: Meta is bankrolling eight nuclear reactors just to keep their data centers running, effectively turning 'Have you tried turning it off and on again?' into a literal fission event. At this rate, the next version of Llama is going to have its own dedicated power grid and a cooling tower in the backyard.
cooking.plain_english: Meta has signed a deal to purchase power from eight new nuclear reactors to support the growing energy demands of its data centers.

cooking.headline: Project Valhalla is finally arriving after a decade-long side quest
cooking.summary: Java's Project Valhalla is finally hitting JDK 28 after ten years of development, which is basically an eternity in tech years. It's the ultimate 'I promise I'm almost there' project that actually managed to show up to the party.
cooking.plain_english: Project Valhalla introduces significant performance improvements to the Java programming language after a decade of development.

cooking.headline: TypeScript 7.0 is here to remind you that your types are still wrong
cooking.summary: TypeScript 7.0 has entered release candidate status, promising to make our complex type definitions even more sophisticated. It's essentially a new set of rules for a game we've been playing for years, just to ensure we spend more time fighting the compiler than writing code.
cooking.plain_english: Microsoft has released a pre-final version of TypeScript 7.0, which introduces new features and improvements to the programming language.

cooking.headline: GLM-5.2 is the smartest model you can't actually run
cooking.summary: GLM-5.2 just dropped as the new king of open models, proving once again that your local GPU is basically just a glorified space heater. It's the ultimate flex until you realize you need a small power plant and a second mortgage to get it to output a single token.
cooking.plain_english: A highly capable artificial intelligence model has been released for public use, though it requires significant computer hardware to operate.

cooking.headline: WebGPU is finally making browsers fast enough to regret our life choices
cooking.summary: Hitting 255 tokens per second on a browser is a massive win for WebGPU, proving we can now run complex AI directly in the tab you forgot to close. It's impressive tech, but it mostly just means your browser can now drain your laptop battery while you're procrastinating on three different things at once.
cooking.plain_english: New software optimizations allow web browsers to run artificial intelligence models significantly faster than before.

cooking.headline: TesterArmy lets AI agents break your code so you don't have to
cooking.summary: We've finally reached the point where we can outsource our QA to AI agents, effectively automating the most tedious part of the group project. It's all fun and games until the AI agent realizes it's being paid to find bugs and starts reporting your entire codebase as a 'feature'.
cooking.plain_english: TesterArmy is a service that uses autonomous AI agents to perform automated testing on web and mobile applications.

cooked.headline: 10,000 GitHub repos are just malware in a trench coat
cooked.summary: Someone scanned the platform and found 10,000 repositories masquerading as legitimate tools while actually being Trojan horses. It turns out the most dangerous 'git clone' command isn't a merge conflict, it's the one that installs a backdoor on your machine.
cooked.plain_english: A security researcher identified 10,000 GitHub repositories that were created to distribute malicious software disguised as useful code.

cooked.headline: The internet's free security blanket just hit the snooze button
cooked.summary: Let's Encrypt had some renewal errors today, briefly putting the entire internet's SSL certificates at risk. It's the digital equivalent of forgetting to pay your rent while you're already standing outside your front door.
cooked.plain_english: Let's Encrypt experienced technical issues that prevented some websites from automatically renewing their security certificates.

cooked.headline: Amazon is investigating employees for expressing concern about AI data centers
cooked.summary: Amazon is launching internal investigations into engineers who publicly questioned the environmental impact of their AI data center expansion. It turns out the only thing the company wants to scale faster than their AI models is their intolerance for internal dissent.
cooked.plain_english: Amazon is reviewing the conduct of employees who publicly criticized the company's plans to increase energy usage for artificial intelligence infrastructure.

cooked.headline: Anthropic's export control drama is the new 'who let the intern push to prod'
cooked.summary: Anthropic is caught in a geopolitical tug-of-war over tech exports, proving that even the smartest AI models can't outrun international trade law. It turns out that 'global expansion' is a lot harder when your hardware is treated like a restricted weapon system.
cooked.plain_english: Anthropic is facing regulatory scrutiny regarding the export of its artificial intelligence technology to international markets.
